steps.title: Distribute to both terms
steps.content: To multiply $9$ by the entire expression in parentheses, multiply $9$ by each term inside. This gives $$9(x+4)=9\cdot x+9\cdot 4$$

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: The first product is $9x$, and the second product is $36$, so the equivalent expression is $$9x+36$$ Choice A leaves $4$ unchanged, choice B adds instead of distributing, and choice C uses the wrong constant. The answer is D.
